title: DOUGLAS COUNTY VETERANS SERVICE OFFICE - 211info
text: DOUGLAS COUNTY VETERANS SERVICE OFFICE DOUGLAS COUNTY VETERANS SERVICE OFFICE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF County Veterans Service Offices (CVSOs) entry points to understanding the benefits, resources, and entitlements available to veterans, their dependents, and survivors. As navigators through USDVA (VA) systems, CVSOs can provide the following: -Veterans' benefits counseling and claims preparation assistance, as well as advocacy throughout the claims process -Counseling on federal VA benefits and other state and federal ancillary benefits -VA Education benefit assistance -Assistance for veterans who wish to enroll in healthcare -Educational benefit claims assistance -Assistance and information about obtaining VA Service-connected and non-service-related disability and survivor benefits -Burial benefits -Information about veteran home loans -Oregon Veterans' Home and ODVA Conservatorship services (financial management for eligible individuals who are legally determined to be "protected persons" under Oregon law) -Speaking engagements to inform and educate about Veterans' benefits 1036 SE Douglas Avenue Room 7/8 Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Veterans, their immediate family members (spouse or un-remarried surviving spouse, child, or stepchild). If a Veteran's discharge is Less than Honorable or General Under Honorable Conditions please e-mail or call for paperwork, guidelines, assistance or referral about Discharge Review or upgrade. Hours: Appointment office hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-11am and 1:30pm-4pm Walk ins: Wednesday 9am-11am and 1:30pm-4pm Email (541) 440-4219 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 440-4219 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call, or e-mail for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: Discharge document (DD214 or WGO form or NGB form) is required by USDVA for new claims. If discharge is unavailable, VSO may assist in obtaining one from National Personnel Records Center in St. Louis MO, or Veteran can visit NARA.gov to obtain a copy online, or if the Veteran ever recorded discharge documents at the County Clerk office, the Clerk can provide a copy. If additional documents are required the VSO will inform the Veteran. AREA SERVED: Douglas County SITE HOURS: Monday-Thursday 9am-11:30am and 1:30pm-4pm TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Located on the Basement Level of the courthouse. Entrance is on the west side of the courthouse (City Hall side). Outreach Sites: Reedsport. Call or email to schedule appointment for these locations. Services: Benefits and Services Assistance * Veteran/Military Personnel Issues Burial Benefits * Veteran/Military Personnel Issues General Health Insurance Information/Counseling * Veteran/Military Personnel Issues Military Records Other Services Offered At This Location: TEMPORARY FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE - OREGON VETERANS EMERGENCY FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE PROGRAM If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. DOUGLAS COUNTY VETERANS SERVICE OFFICE
